body.title: Device skills fold into uu device <dev> <verb> [<noun>] + /device <dev> <verb>
body.intention: I intend that device-specific behavior lives with the device, reached by a uniform device verb, not as bespoke top-level skills.
body.description:
**Problem:** device-specific skills (readigor, igor-diagnose, critic-launch, ...) clutter the top-level skill space; each device reinvents its own surface, and igor's skills wrongly sit in ~/.claude instead of with igor.

**Shape (DISPATCH MECHANISM — first verb is separate):** uniform `uu device <dev> <verb> [<noun>]` (zero-inference CLI) and `/device <dev> <verb> [<noun>]` (CC-facing). `/device` is a THIN SHIM over `uu device` for the script case — one dispatch path. Each device's shim carries two folders:
  - `devices/<dev>/bin/`  — zero-inference executor scripts (the view layer at the device boundary)
  - `devices/<dev>/skills/` — reasoning-bearing skills the CC executes (workflow layer)
FAILOVER RESOLUTION: caller gives ONE verb; dispatcher resolves it — `/device` checks skills/ then bin/; `uu device` serves bin/ only (a skills/-only verb from the bare CLI errors with a pointer to /device). Caller never says 'skill' vs 'command'. UNIQUE-NAME RULE: a verb name must be unique across bin/+skills/ per device — dispatcher errors at load if a name is in both (no silent precedence). igor skills MOVE OUT of ~/.claude INTO devices/igor/{bin,skills}/ (skills travel with the device; devices/ one-subdir-per-device, independently deployable). This is the two-products split scoped to the device boundary.

**Affected files:** uu CLI dispatcher (extends T-uu-cli-dispatcher with a `device` verb); devices/<dev>/{bin,skills}/ (igor as pilot); /device skill (thin shim); skills/ device-specific dirs (migrate then delete).
**Design rules:** device.py/shim.py are the design center — device verbs dispatch through the device; devices/ one subdir per device, independently deployable.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the dispatch mechanism (uu device + /device shim + bin/skills failover + unique-name load check) and migrating igor's folder structure as pilot. OUT — individual verbs (feed = T-uu-readfeed, gated on this; diagnose/critic = follow-ons).
**Test plan:** a trivial probe verb in devices/igor/bin/ resolves via `uu device igor <probe>`; a probe in devices/igor/skills/ resolves via `/device igor <probe>`; a name placed in BOTH folders makes the dispatcher error at load (unique-name proof, red->green).
**Completion criteria:** uu device + /device dispatch works for igor (pilot) across bin/ and skills/; unique-name collision errors at load; >=1 former device-skill's structure migrated into devices/igor/.
**NOTE (L):** advisor() reviewed 2026-06-24 — design (bin/+skills/+failover+unique-name) confirmed sound.
body.result: Built the uu device <dev> <verb> dispatch mechanism (the two-products split at the device boundary) + /device CC-facing shim. uu_device.py resolves a verb against devices/<dev>/{bin,skills}/: bin/ → exec the zero-inference script; skills/-only from the bare CLI → error pointing at /device (needs CC); verb in BOTH → load error (unique-name rule, no silent precedence); unknown → error listing verbs. bin/uu gained a 'device' case (inherits first-token + $@ passthrough). /device skill = thin shim (skills/ then bin/ failover, one dispatch path). igor PILOT migrated out of ~/.claude into devices/igor/: skills/diagnose (← igor-diagnose, cleaned of dead creds/paths + dropped /debug-pe-chain), skills/map (← map-igor, BROKEN/rebuild stamp — its map_igor.py never existed), bin/state (NEW genuine zero-inference liveness view). Deleted ~/.claude copies of igor-diagnose + map-igor. Live-verified: 'uu device igor state' runs (bin/), diagnose/map resolve as skills, a both-folders clash is a load error. readigor left for T-uu-readfeed (its owner). DEFERRED (igor-last follow-ons): deep content-merge of diagnose, rebuild of map as devices/igor/bin/map against the fs store. Proof: tests/test_uu_device.py (hermetic dispatch contract incl. unique-name red->green @ 62b1194e).
